methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Curio Solutions is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Ed McGinnis and Pete Zweifel that has raised $16M+. The valuation analysis draws from a nuclear energy peer universe of 11 companies: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology applies directly to this cohort. Using the $16M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $32M. The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables; while 8 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide medians unreliable for this analysis.

The single applicable method produces a triangulated range of $27M to $37M, with a median of $32M. This range reflects the stage-typical expansion multiple from committed capital. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the crowded advanced nuclear landscape, nearterm funding runway and burn profile, and regulatory or commercialization milestones that could meaningfully reset valuation expectations at the next fundraising round.
